title: lavandula mairei var. mairei
intro: Lavandula mairei var. mairei, also known as Maire's lavender, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lavender family (Lamiaceae). It is native to the southwestern coast of France and is known for its distinctive purple flowers and calming properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for soothing skin irritations and wounds. Health benefits of Maire's lavender include reducing anxiety and promoting better sleep, and it can be prepared as a tea, essential oil, or infused oil for various herbal preparations.
